title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Old Crickenshaw leads Lucy to a large black oak piece of furniture brought from Lichfield. He shows her a secret moneybox containing gold sovereigns, telling her that no one, including Emma, knows about this collection. He claims he is showing her to prove he is not weak and offers her advice on waiting to control her future. Lucy leaves when Emma calls for her, feeling uncertain about the nature of the encounter.

At Scotland Yard, Inspector Cladock discusses the murder investigation with a French counterpart over the phone. The French officer notes that identifying strangled women is difficult and that many travelers go unnoticed. Emma Crikesopp arrives at the office to report information regarding the murder at Latherford Manor.

Emma presents a faded letter from her brother Edmund, who died during the war. In the letter, Edmund mentions his intention to marry a French girl named Martine. Emma explains that she could find no military records of the marriage and assumed the union never occurred. However, a month ago, Emma received a letter from a woman named Martina Crickenshaw. The letter, written in French script, claims that Martina and Edmund were married and that they have a son. Martina states she will be in Britain the following week and asks to visit. Emma reveals that, following her brother Harold's advice to be cautious, she invited the woman to Latherford. Shortly after, Emma received a telegram from "Matilda" stating she had to return to France unexpectedly, and there has been no contact since.
